creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Zane C. Bowers-Hadley from comment #24)
> Why is UBLIO still the default option after being broken
Because disabling it cripples performance and breaks actual usage (vs. just mkntfs) in another subtle way (see bug #264196).  There were plans to fix caching properly and remove the option altogether, but AFAIK we're not there yet.
